title: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled

description: Experience the rich, smoky flavors of authentic Tandoori Chicken, perfectly cooked on a charcoal grill. This classic Indian dish features succulent chicken marinated in a vibrant blend of yogurt and spices, char-grilled to tender perfection. A delightful culinary journey for your dinner table, bringing the essence of Indian street food to your home.

ingredients.notes: cut into 8-10 pieces, skin removed, deep slits made

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Wash the chicken pieces thoroughly and pat them dry. Make deep slits all over the chicken pieces, ensuring they penetrate the flesh to allow the marinade to seep in. This helps in even cooking and flavor absorption.

instructions.title: First Marination
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste, 1 tsp red chili powder, 0.5 tsp turmeric powder, 1 tsp salt, and 2 tbsp lemon juice. Rub this mixture generously all over the chicken pieces, making sure to get into the slits. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 1 hour.

instructions.title: Second Marination
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, whisk together the thick plain yogurt, remaining 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste, 2 tbsp tandoori masala, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tbsp crushed kasuri methi, 2 tbsp mustard oil, and a pinch of red food color (if using).

instructions.title: Combine and Marinate Further
instructions.text: Add the chicken from the first marination to the second marinade. Mix well, ensuring every piece of chicken is thoroughly coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for a minimum of 4 hours, or ideally overnight, for the best flavor and tenderness.

instructions.title: Prepare the Charcoal Grill
instructions.text: About 30 minutes before grilling, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the charcoal to create a medium-hot cooking zone. The coals should be glowing red with a light ash coating. If using skewers, thread the chicken pieces onto them, ensuring they are secure.

instructions.title: Grill the Tandoori Chicken
instructions.text: Place the marinated chicken pieces directly on the hot grill grates or over the charcoal if using skewers. Grill for 25-35 minutes, turning every 5-7 minutes to ensure even cooking and charring. Baste the chicken with butter or oil during the last 10 minutes of cooking for added moisture and flavor. Cook until the chicken is tender, cooked through, and has beautiful char marks.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the Tandoori Chicken from the grill. Let it rest for a few minutes before serving. Garnish with fresh cilantro and lemon wedges. Serve hot with mint chutney, onion rings, or naan bread.

tips: For truly authentic flavor, ensure you use hung yogurt (dahi). To make it, place plain yogurt in a muslin cloth and hang it for 2-3 hours to drain excess water.
Do not skip the double marination process; it is crucial for tender, flavorful Tandoori chicken. The first marinade tenderizes, and the second infuses deep spice flavors.

Maintain a medium-hot grill and turn the chicken frequently to prevent burning while ensuring even cooking. Basting with butter or oil towards the end adds a delicious sheen and keeps the chicken moist.
article:
Mastering the art of Indian cuisine at home can seem daunting, but some dishes are so universally loved and deceptively simple that they become instant favorites. Tandoori Chicken is undoubtedly one of them. While often associated with restaurant dining, recreating that smoky, succulent perfection in your backyard is not only possible but incredibly rewarding. When done over hot coals, this classic Indian dinner recipe transforms into something truly spectacular. Get ready to impress your family and friends with an authentic **Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken Recipe** that bursts with flavor and aroma.

Tandoori Chicken, with its vibrant red hue and intoxicating aroma, is a cornerstone of North Indian cuisine. Its origins trace back to the clay tandoor oven, where marinated chicken is cooked at high temperatures, resulting in a unique smoky char and juicy tenderness. Replicating this experience at home, especially with a charcoal grill, brings you remarkably close to the traditional taste. The smoky kiss of the charcoal imparts a depth of flavor that simply cannot be achieved with an oven or a gas grill, making this an essential technique for any home cook aspiring to authentic Indian flavors.

## What is Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled?

At its heart, Tandoori Chicken is a dish of marinated chicken, traditionally cooked in a tandoor, a cylindrical clay oven. The marinade is a creamy, spicy blend of yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, and a vibrant mix of spices like Kashmiri chili powder, turmeric, cumin, coriander, and garam masala. This yogurt base not only tenderizes the chicken but also helps the spices adhere and penetrate deeply, infusing every bite with flavor.

When we talk about **Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken**, we're specifically referring to cooking this marinated chicken over an open flame fueled by charcoal. This method is the closest you can get to the tandoor experience without owning one. The intense, radiant heat from the charcoal cooks the chicken quickly, sealing in juices, while the smoke imparts that signature smoky flavor and slightly charred exterior. Unlike gas grills, charcoal provides a more even, sustained heat and the essential element of smoke, which is crucial for achieving that authentic tandoori taste. It’s a sensory experience – the sizzle, the aroma, and the eventual taste – that truly elevates this Indian dinner recipe.

## Ingredients You Need

To make an unforgettable **Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken Recipe**, quality ingredients are key. Here’s what you’ll need:

*   **For the Chicken:**
    *   2.5-3 lbs (1.2-1.5 kg) whole chicken, cut into 8 pieces (or 6-8 bone-in, skin-on chicken thighs/drumsticks)
    *   2 tablespoons lemon juice
    *   1 teaspoon salt (for initial rub)
*   **For the Marinade:**
    *   1 cup (240g) full-fat plain yogurt (Greek yogurt works well for thickness)
    *   2 tablespoons ginger-garlic paste
    *   2 tablespoons Kashmiri chili powder (for color and mild heat, or use regular chili powder for more heat)
    *   1 teaspoon turmeric powder
    *   1 tablespoon ground cumin
    *   1 tablespoon ground coriander
    *   1 teaspoon garam masala
    *   1/2 teaspoon black pepper
    *   1 teaspoon salt (adjust to taste)
    *   1/2 teaspoon red food coloring (optional, for authentic restaurant color)
    *   1 tablespoon mustard oil (optional, for pungent flavor) or vegetable oil
*   **For Basting:**
    *   2-3 tablespoons melted butter or ghee

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps to create a show-stopping **Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken Recipe**:

1.  **Prepare the Chicken:** Wash and pat the chicken pieces thoroughly dry. Make deep gashes (2-3 per piece) into the thickest parts of the chicken. This allows the marinade to penetrate deeply. Rub the chicken all over with 2 tablespoons of lemon juice and 1 teaspoon of salt. Set aside for 15-20 minutes.
2.  **Make the Marinade:** In a large bowl, combine the yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, Kashmiri chili powder, turmeric, cumin, coriander, garam masala, black pepper, and 1 teaspoon of salt. If using, add the red food coloring and mustard oil. Mix everything thoroughly until a smooth, vibrant paste forms.
3.  **Marinate the Chicken:** Add the chicken pieces to the marinade, ensuring each piece is fully coated, working the marinade into the gashes. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 4-6 hours, or ideally overnight (12-24 hours) for maximum flavor and tenderness.
4.  **Prepare the Charcoal Grill:** About 30-45 minutes before grilling, light your charcoal. Arrange the coals for two-zone cooking – a hotter side for searing and a cooler side for indirect cooking. The ideal temperature for grilling Tandoori Chicken is medium-high heat (around 375-400°F or 190-200°C).
5.  **Grill the Chicken:** Remove the chicken from the refrigerator 30 minutes before grilling to bring it closer to room temperature. Lightly oil your grill grates to prevent sticking. Place the chicken pieces over the direct heat side of the grill. Sear for 2-3 minutes per side until you get some nice char marks.
6.  **Cook Indirectly:** Move the chicken to the indirect heat side of the grill. Close the lid and cook for 20-30 minutes, flipping every 5-7 minutes.
7.  **Baste and Finish:** During the last 10-15 minutes of cooking, baste the chicken generously with melted butter or ghee. Continue cooking until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C) at the thickest part, and the juices run clear. You can move it back to direct heat briefly at the end for a final char if desired.
8.  **Rest and Serve:** Transfer the cooked Tandoori Chicken to a platter, cover loosely with foil, and let it rest for 5-10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring tender, moist chicken.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving restaurant-quality Tandoori Chicken at home is all about these finer details:

*   **Marination Time is Gold:** The longer you marinate, the better. Overnight (12-24 hours) is highly recommended for the yogurt and spices to truly tenderize the chicken and infuse it with flavor.
*   **Bone-In, Skin-On Chicken:** While boneless chicken can be used, bone-in, skin-on pieces offer superior flavor and moisture. The bone helps conduct heat, and the skin protects the meat from drying out, even if you don't eat it.
*   **Deep Gashes:** Don't be shy with scoring the chicken. Deep cuts allow the marinade to penetrate the meat, ensuring flavor in every bite.
*   **Control Your Heat:** Charcoal grilling requires attention. Maintain a consistent medium-high heat. If the chicken starts to char too quickly, move it to the cooler side of the grill. Flare-ups can be managed by moving the chicken or sprinkling water on the coals.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Grill:** Cook in batches if necessary. Overcrowding lowers the grill temperature and steams the chicken instead of grilling it, preventing a good char.
*   **Basting is Key:** The butter or ghee basting not only adds flavor but also helps create that beautiful, slightly crisp exterior and keeps the chicken moist.
*   **Use a Thermometer:** For perfectly cooked, safe chicken, an instant-read thermometer is your best friend. Chicken is done at 165°F (74°C).

## Serving Suggestions

A perfectly cooked **Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken Recipe** deserves to be served with accompaniments that complement its rich flavors.
*   **Naan or Roti:** Warm, fluffy naan bread or whole wheat roti are perfect for scooping up the chicken and its juices.
*   **Mint Chutney:** A refreshing mint and coriander chutney provides a cool contrast to the spicy chicken.
*   **Lemon Wedges and Onion Rings:** A squeeze of fresh lemon juice brightens the flavors, and thinly sliced raw onion rings add a pleasant crunch.
*   **Kachumber Salad:** A simple Indian salad of diced cucumber, tomato, onion, and a squeeze of lime juice is a light and refreshing side.
*   **Basmati Rice:** A side of fragrant basmati rice or jeera rice (cumin rice) can make it a more substantial meal.
*   **Dal or Curry:** For a grand Indian feast, serve Tandoori Chicken alongside a comforting lentil dal or a mild curry.

## Variations and Substitutions

This versatile **Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken Recipe** can be adapted to suit different preferences and dietary needs:

*   **Vegetarian Options:** The marinade works beautifully with paneer (Indian cheese), cauliflower florets, mushrooms, or even thick slices of bell peppers and zucchini. Skewer them and grill until tender and lightly charred.
*   **Different Chicken Cuts:** Boneless chicken thighs or breasts can be used, but adjust grilling time accordingly (they will cook faster). Cut boneless chicken into 1.5-inch pieces for Tandoori Chicken Tikka.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of Kashmiri chili powder for color and heat. For more heat, add a pinch of cayenne pepper or a finely chopped green chili to the marinade.
*   **Marinade Boosters:** For an even richer marinade, consider adding a tablespoon of cashew paste or a touch of heavy cream.
*   **No Charcoal Grill?** While charcoal offers the best flavor, you can still make Tandoori Chicken using other methods:
    *   **Gas Grill:** Follow the same steps, using two-zone cooking. You can add wood chips in a smoker box for a hint of smoky flavor.
    *   **Oven:** Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Place chicken on a wire rack over a baking sheet. Bake for 25-35 minutes, flipping halfway, until cooked through. For char, finish under the broiler for a few minutes, watching carefully to prevent burning.
    *   **Air Fryer:** Cook at 375°F (190°C) for 18-25 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden and cooked.

Bringing the authentic flavors of an Indian tandoor to your backyard with a charcoal grill is a culinary adventure worth taking. This **Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken Recipe** is more than just a meal; it's an experience that tantalizes the senses and delivers a taste of India right to your table. So fire up those coals, prepare your marinade, and get ready to enjoy one of the most beloved Indian dinner recipes, perfected in your own home.
